stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant open-source large language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML domains, including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Llama indicates relevance to the competitive landscape of large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: White House drastically shortens deadline for dropping quantum-vulnerable crypto
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: The White House has accelerated the deadline for transitioning to quantum-resistant cryptography.
The urgency stems from national security risks posed by current crypto systems to future quantum computers.
This signals a major, government-driven shift in cybersecurity standards across critical infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI, indicating its primary function.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also delivers faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, signaling ongoing optimization efforts.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring data processing occurs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Oracle is undertaking massive, debt-fueled investments in data center infrastructure to power its AI initiatives.
The company is simultaneously implementing significant workforce reductions, with 21,000 layoffs reported.
The story highlights the tension between large-scale AI spending and corporate restructuring within major tech firms.

stories.title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
stories.author: Mac Schwager
bullet_points: Introduces 'InSight,' a framework designed to enable autonomous skill acquisition in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.
The core innovation is making VLAs 'steerable' at the primitive-action level (e.g., specific actions like 'lift upward'), overcoming limitations of fixed training data.
The framework utilizes an automated segmentation pipeline to partition raw demonstrations into labeled, reusable primitive actions.

stories.title: OpenThoughts-Agent: Data Recipes for Agentic Models
stories.author: Ludwig Schmidt
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI: the lack of publicly known methods for curating diverse training data for general-purpose agentic models.
Introduces OpenThoughts-Agent (OT-Agent), a fully open data curation pipeline designed to train models capable of generalizing across various agentic tasks.
Contrasts with existing efforts (like SWE-Smith, SERA) that often focus too narrowly on single benchmarks.

stories.title: IV-CoT: Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought for Structure-Aware Text-to-Image Generation
stories.author: Zhenan Sun
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in current text-to-image models: poor structure-aware prompt following (e.g., object counts, spatial relations).
Proposes IV-CoT (Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought) to decouple structural planning from appearance rendering.
Aims to improve the fidelity and adherence to complex instructions in unified multi-modal large language models (MLLMs).

stories.title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
stories.author: Mac Schwager
bullet_points: Introduces 'InSight,' a framework designed to overcome the limitations of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models in skill acquisition.
InSight enables autonomous skill acquisition by making VLAs 'steerable' at the primitive-action level (e.g., specific movements like 'lift upward').
The framework utilizes an automated segmentation pipeline to partition demonstrations into labeled, actionable primitives.

stories.title: Grad Detect: Gradient-Based Hallucination Detection in LLMs
stories.author: Brent M. Werness
bullet_points: Introduces 'Grad Detect,' a novel gradient-based method for detecting hallucinations in Large Language Models (LLMs).
The technique analyzes layer-wise gradient patterns from a single forward-backward pass during inference.
Addresses a critical issue for LLM deployment: ensuring reliability by mitigating the risk of generated hallucinations.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It strengthens Ollama's MLX engine specifically for Apple silicon users.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Google Home will soon get better at recognizing you
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google is updating Google Home to improve facial recognition accuracy.
The new feature aims to better identify tagged individuals even when they are not fully facing the camera.
The update is scheduled to roll out starting June 23rd.

stories.title: FLUX3D: High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Generation with Diffusion-Aligned Sparse Representation
stories.author: Hengkai Guo
bullet_points: FLUX3D addresses limitations in high-fidelity 3D Gaussian generation using sparse voxel representations.
The research tackles two bottlenecks: the use of semantic 2D features that suppress fine visual details, and limitations in standard diffusion transformers.
It proposes a novel approach to improve the preservation of high-frequency visual details during the 3D reconstruction process.

stories.title: World Models in Pieces: Structural Certification for General Agents
stories.author: Tongxin Li
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard guarantees for general AI agents operating in complex environments ('big-world regime').
It formally proves that general agents cannot be universally capable, invalidating traditional worst-case analysis.
A novel approach called 'structural certification,' a transition-local framework, is introduced to certify agent capabilities.

stories.title: Grading the Grader: Lessons from Evaluating an Agentic Data Analysis System
stories.author: Kai-Tai Hsu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the difficulty of evaluating complex, agentic data analysis systems due to their rich, multi-faceted outputs (code, results, diagnostics).
A key research focus is distinguishing genuine output disagreement from artifacts introduced by the automated grading process.
The study investigates automated grader reliability and proposes strategies to improve grading quality using a multi-agent system (LAMBDA).

stories.title: Accuracy and Satisfaction in Multi-Turn LLM Dialogues for NFR Assessment
stories.author: Sepideh Ghanavati
bullet_points: Current LLM evaluation benchmarks primarily focus on functional correctness, neglecting conversational quality.
There is a critical gap in assessing how LLMs handle Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs) in dialogue.
The research proposes new methods needed to evaluate collaborative reasoning about vague and context-dependent NFRs.

stories.title: Real vs. Complex Spectral Bases for Neural Operators: The Role of Green's Function Alignment
stories.author: Sathya Ravi
bullet_points: Introduces the Hartley Neural Operator (HNO) as a real-valued alternative to Fourier Neural Operators (FNO).
HNO replaces the complex Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) with the purely real Discrete Hartley Transform.
This modification aims to eliminate representational redundancy inherent in using complex FFTs for real-valued PDE solutions.

stories.title: L3Cube-MahaPOS: A Marathi Part-of-Speech Tagging Dataset and BERT Models
stories.author: Raviraj Joshi
bullet_points: The research introduces L3Cube-MahaPOS, a new Part-of-Speech (POS) tagging dataset specifically for the Marathi language.
Marathi is highlighted as an under-resourced language in NLP, despite having a large global speaker base.
The work addresses computational challenges posed by Marathi's rich morphology and flexible word order using BERT models.

stories.title: FlowPipe: LLM-Enhanced Conditional Generative Flow Networks for Data Preparation Pipeline Construction
stories.author: Yanwei Yu
bullet_points: Introduces FlowPipe, an LLM-enhanced method for automatically constructing data preparation pipelines.
Addresses the computational difficulty of pipeline construction due to combinatorial operator sequences and expensive evaluation.
Overcomes limitations of existing Multi-DQN methods, specifically regarding weak long-horizon credit assignment.

stories.title: Less is More: Quality-Aware Training Data Selection for Scientific Summarization
stories.author: Grigorios Tsoumakas
bullet_points: Addresses the issue of low quality and misalignment in existing scientific summarization datasets (using abstracts as gold standards).
Contributes a large-scale, newly constructed biomedical and life science dataset for long-document summarization.
Aims to improve training for modern long-context models by providing better, more robust data.

stories.title: SHERLOC: Structured Diagnostic Localization for Code Repair Agents
stories.author: Boris Ginsburg
bullet_points: Introduces SHERLOC, a training-free framework designed to improve fault localization for code repair LLM agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current localization methods: they provide locations without the necessary diagnostic context for repair agents.
SHERLOC pairs a reasoning LLM with compact representations to enable structured, hypothesis-driven exploration.

stories.title: Paying to Know: Micro-Transaction Markets for Verified Product Information in Agentic E-Commerce
stories.author: Matthew Shardlow
bullet_points: The paper proposes a shift in e-commerce focus from product matching to information acquisition in agent-driven systems.
It argues that autonomous buyer agents make exhaustive product investigation possible, making trustworthy data the new bottleneck.
The solution envisioned is an 'agentic e-commerce' model utilizing micro-transaction markets for verified product information.

stories.title: Are We Ready For An Agent-Native Memory System?
stories.author: Fan Wu
bullet_points: LLM agent memory is evolving beyond simple retrieval into complex data management systems supporting storage, retrieval, and lifecycle governance.
Current evaluations of agent memory are criticized for treating the underlying system as a black box, relying only on end-to-end task success metrics.
The paper suggests a need for more granular evaluation methods to properly assess the capabilities of advanced agent memory systems.

stories.title: Posterior Refinement: Fast Language Generation via Any-Order Flow Maps
stories.author: Nicholas M. Boffi
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in non-autoregressive language generation, specifically the factorization error in Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs).
It introduces a method leveraging Flow Map Language Models (FMLMs) to enable iterative refinement in generation.
The core concept involves using joint sequence transport to overcome bottlenecks and achieve high-quality, fast language generation.

stories.title: Qwen-AgentWorld: Language World Models for General Agents
stories.author: ilreb
bullet_points: Introduction of Qwen-AgentWorld, a new framework for Language World Models.
Focuses on developing general-purpose agents capable of interacting within simulated environments.
Represents a step toward more sophisticated, embodied AI agents.

stories.title: The 80/20 Rule of AI Code — Why the Last 20% Takes 80% of Your Time
stories.author: harsh2644
bullet_points: The article discusses the practical limitations of current AI code generation tools.
It highlights that while AI can handle the initial, straightforward parts of coding quickly (the 80%), the complex parts require significant human effort.
The core theme is the shift in developer focus from writing boilerplate code to refining and debugging the 'last 20%' of the implementation.

stories.title: Agents write code, but they don't remember
stories.author: lizziepika
bullet_points: The article argues that while AI agents are proficient at code generation, they currently lack persistent memory.
It suggests a fundamental shift in the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), where intent drives development rather than code.
The author highlights the risks associated with agents whose reasoning processes are ephemeral or disappear during execution.

stories.title: Hetzner Doubled Its Prices Again. The AI Memory Crunch Is Why
stories.author: devopsdaily
bullet_points: Hetzner, a hosting provider, has increased its service prices again.
The primary driver cited for the price hikes is the increasing demand and constraints related to AI memory.
This indicates a growing infrastructure strain caused by the expansion of AI workloads.
